dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 5184 RH: Affordable Housing Over Mandating Efficiency Standards Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Houchin (for herself and Mr. Flood) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce
Additional sponsors: Mr. Shreve, Mr. Auchincloss, Mr. Edwards, and Mr. Goldman of Texas
Reported with amendments, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
action.action-instruction: Strike out all after the enacting clause and insert the part printed in italic
For text of introduced bill, see copy of bill as introduced on September 8, 2025

form.official-title: To prohibit the Secretary of Energy from enforcing energy efficiency standards applicable to manufactured housing, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Affordable Housing Over Mandating Efficiency Standards Act or the Affordable HOMES Act.

section.header: Recommendations for revisions to standards for manufactured housing

subsection.text: Section 413 of the Energy Independence and Security Act of 2007 (42 U.S.C. 17071) is amended—

paragraph.text: by amending subsection (a) to read as follows:

subsection.text: The Secretary may transmit to the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development recommendations for revisions to the preemptive energy conservation standards established under section 604(g) of the Housing and Community Development Act of 1974 (42 U.S.C. 5403(g)).

paragraph.text: by amending subsection (b) to read as follows:

subsection.text: Any recommendations for revisions to a preemptive energy conservation standard transmitted under this section shall—

paragraph.text: be based on a determination of the cost-effectiveness of such standard as proposed to be revised, taking into consideration life cycle construction and operating costs;
include estimates of the impact of such standard as proposed to be revised on the initial purchase price of manufactured homes; and

subparagraph.text: factory construction techniques and limitations unique to manufactured homes;
climate zones established by the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development;
alternative methods that achieve equivalent or improved energy performance as compared to such standard as proposed to be revised; and
estimated payback periods for any added costs arising from such standard as proposed to be revised.

subsection.text: The final rule titled Energy Conservation Program: Energy Conservation Standards for Manufactured Housing published by the Department of Energy in the Federal Register on May 31, 2022 (87 Fed. Reg. 32728), shall have no force or effect.

bill.official-title-amendment: Amend the title so as to read: A bill to authorize the Secretary of Energy to transmit to the Secretary of Housing and Urban Development recommendations for changes to preemptive energy conservation standards applicable to manufactured homes..

endorsement.action-desc: Reported with amendments, committed to the Committee of the Whole House on the State of the Union, and ordered to be printed
